_meta.description: Curated CrPC (1973) -> BNSS (Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023) correspondence for the procedural provisions citizens and practitioners most commonly cite (FIR, arrest, bail, maintenance, etc.).
_meta.transition_rule: Trials/procedure for matters on or after 2024-07-01 follow the BNSS. Proceedings already underway / matters before that date continue under the CrPC.
_meta.source: Compiled from publicly published CrPC<->BNSS correspondence tables (MHA / legislative summaries).
_meta.verification_note: VERIFY every entry against the official Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 bare act before relying on it.

41.offence: When police may arrest without a warrant

50.offence: Person arrested to be informed of grounds of arrest and right to bail

125.offence: Order for maintenance of wife, children and parents

144.offence: Power to issue order in urgent cases of nuisance or apprehended danger

154.offence: Information in cognizable cases (registration of FIR)

156.offence: Police officer's power to investigate cognizable case
156.note: Magistrate's power to order investigation: CrPC 156(3) -> BNSS 175(3).

164.offence: Recording of confessions and statements by a Magistrate

437.offence: When bail may be taken in case of non-bailable offence

438.offence: Direction for grant of bail to person apprehending arrest (anticipatory bail)

439.offence: Special powers of High Court or Court of Session regarding bail

482.offence: Saving of inherent powers of the High Court
